key_insight: A frozen language-model backbone with LoRA-based continual learning, especially hard-routed mixture-of-experts, can add new motion concepts over time while preserving both motion-to-text and text-to-motion performance.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical path for lifelong motion-language agents in robotics, animation, and human-computer interaction where new gestures or movement styles must be learned without retraining from scratch.
method: Sequential continual learning on a frozen LLM backbone
LoRA variants with autoencoder-based routing to task-specific experts
Hard expert selection at inference to reduce inter-task interference
evidence_signal: The authors benchmarked on a reproducible five-task HumanML3D-derived sequence and report near-zero forgetting with strong generation and captioning quality in both directions.
watch_item: The gap between token-level accuracy and downstream motion quality means routine language metrics alone may miss failures, so evaluation must include task-level generation and captioning measures.
paper_title: Towards Continual Motion-Language Agents: LoRA Variants for Incremental Motion Understanding and Generation
